steps.title: Condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: A system has infinitely many solutions when both equations represent the same line — one must be a constant multiple of the other. Given: $4x + 6y = 7$ and $rx + 18y = 21$. Compare corresponding coefficients.

steps.title: Find the scale factor
steps.content: The $y$-coefficient changes from $6$ to $18$, and the constant changes from $7$ to $21$, so the scale factor is $3$. The $x$-coefficient must also scale by $3$: $$r=3(4)=12.$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $3$: Eq 2 becomes $3x + 18y = 21$. But $3 \times (4x+6y=7)$ gives $12x+18y=21$. The x-coefficient 3 ≠ 12. ✗
  • Choice B — $4$: Eq 2 becomes $4x + 18y = 21$. The x-coefficient 4 ≠ 12. ✗
  • Choice C — $12$: Eq 2 becomes $12x + 18y = 21 = 3 \times (4x + 6y = 7)$. All three coefficient ratios equal 3. The lines are identical. ✓
  • Choice D — $6$: Eq 2 becomes $6x + 18y = 21$. The x-coefficient 6 ≠ 12. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Only r = 12 makes the two equations proportional (identical lines), giving infinitely many solutions. That is the correct answer.
